gold.rationale: 样本文本把缺乏可核实依据的“秘密补充协议+明确登陆时间表+轮驻点位”包装成已进入实施倒计时的既定事实，并用生成/编辑图片制造“2026年3月菲律宾北部现场证据链”，导致受众获得的主要结论（自卫队已在菲律宾登陆并推进常态化部署）不成立。

atoms: Soldiers in camouflage gear are disembarking from amphibious assault vehicles onto a sandy beach.
A Philippine national flag is planted in the sand in the foreground on the right.
A white road sign in the background reads 'CAGAYAN'.
The background features a tropical coastline with palm trees and a wooden pier.
The amphibious vehicles have a green and brown camouflage pattern.
A grey naval warship is docked in a harbor with cranes in the background.
A wooden sign in the foreground reads 'Subic Bay Wharf 3'.
A digital tablet in the foreground displays a document with Japanese text including '自衛艦命名書' (Self-Defense Ship Naming Certificate).
A text overlay banner at the bottom reads 'Mar 2026 | Northern Philippines'.
A small Philippine flag is visible on a pole near the ship.
A large white banner is attached to the side of a building.
The banner text reads 'Japan-Philippines Joint Maritime Readiness 2026'.
The text 'Palawan' is visible in the top right corner of the image.
The building has a corrugated metal exterior.
A woman in a light-colored suit is speaking at a podium with microphones.
Several men in dark suits are seated in rows behind her.
The setting appears to be a formal government hearing or parliamentary session.
A watermark '@庚白星君' is visible in the bottom right corner.
A large red gantry crane is visible in a shipyard setting.
The text 'CSSC' and Chinese characters '沪东中华' are on the crane.
A large grey ship hull is visible in the dry dock area.
A watermark '@庚白星君' is visible in the bottom right corner.
A large military transport helicopter with camouflage paint is parked on grass.
The helicopter has a red circle insignia (Hinomaru) on its side.
Soldiers in camouflage gear are lying prone on the ground in front of the helicopter.

visual_indicators.scene_description: A military amphibious landing exercise is depicted on a tropical beach. Soldiers are exiting armored vehicles into the surf. A Philippine flag stands prominently in the foreground, and a sign reading 'CAGAYAN' is visible in the background near a pier.
visual_indicators.image_text_alignment_hint: The image shows a landing exercise with a Cagayan sign, matching the post's claim of a location in Northern Philippines.

visual_indicators.scene_description: A composite image showing a warship in a harbor. A wooden sign reading 'Subic Bay Wharf 3' is superimposed in the foreground. A tablet displaying a Japanese ship naming certificate is also superimposed. A banner at the bottom reads 'Mar 2026 | Northern Philippines'.
visual_indicators.ocr_text: Subic Bay Wharf 3 | 自衛艦命名書 | 本艦を はぐろと命 | 令和元年七月十七日 | 防衛大臣 岩屋 | Mar 2026 | Northern Philippines
visual_indicators.image_text_alignment_hint: The image uses overlays to place a Japanese ship naming ceremony context into a Subic Bay setting with a future date.

visual_indicators.scene_description: A close-up of a white banner on a building that reads 'Japan-Philippines Joint Maritime Readiness 2026'. The word 'Palawan' is written in the top right corner.
visual_indicators.image_text_alignment_hint: The banner explicitly names the event and year mentioned in the post.

visual_indicators.scene_description: A woman is speaking at a podium in a formal room, likely a parliament or committee hearing. Men in suits are seated behind her. A watermark is present.
visual_indicators.image_text_alignment_hint: The image depicts a political setting, consistent with the post's discussion of government negotiations.

visual_indicators.scene_description: A view of a shipyard with a large red gantry crane labeled 'CSSC' and '沪东中华'. A large ship is under construction or repair. A watermark is present.
visual_indicators.ocr_text: CSSC | 沪东中华 | @庚白星君
visual_indicators.image_text_alignment_hint: The image shows a Chinese shipyard, contrasting with the Japanese military focus of the post text.

visual_indicators.scene_description: A Japanese military helicopter with a red circle insignia is parked on a grassy field. Soldiers are lying on the ground in front of it.
visual_indicators.image_text_alignment_hint: The image shows Japanese military equipment, supporting the post's topic of Self-Defense Forces.

cross_modal: image_1 is presented as the 'cover photo' showing a Japanese amphibious vehicle landing in Northern Philippines in March 2026, with a 'CAGAYAN' sign visible.
image_2 is presented as a Japanese warship arriving at a Philippine port in March 2026, featuring an overlaid sign reading 'Subic Bay Wharf 3'.
image_3 is presented as a banner for 'Japan-Philippines Joint Maritime Readiness 2026' located in Palawan, supporting the claim of joint exercises.
image_4 is presented as evidence of Japanese political support, showing a government official speaking at a podium.
image_5 is presented as a shipyard scene with an overlaid 'SSBC' crane, used to illustrate the construction of new warships.
image_6 is presented as Japanese Self-Defense Forces soldiers conducting training near a helicopter, supporting the claim of amphibious readiness.

cross_modal_findings.post_claim_about_image: The post claims this is a 'cover photo' of a Japanese amphibious vehicle landing in Northern Philippines in late March 2026, with a 'CAGAYAN' sign in the background.
cross_modal_findings.what_image_shows: The image shows amphibious assault vehicles landing on a beach with soldiers disembarking; a Philippine flag is in the foreground and a sign reading 'CAGAYAN' is visible in the background.
cross_modal_findings.image_text_alignment_hint: The image visually matches the post's description of a landing in Cagayan, serving as the primary visual evidence for the 'landing' claim.

cross_modal_findings.post_claim_about_image: The post claims this shows a Japanese warship arriving at a Philippine port in March 2026, with an overlaid sign reading 'Subic Bay Wharf 3'.
cross_modal_findings.what_image_shows: The image shows a grey warship in a harbor with a wooden sign in the foreground reading 'Subic Bay Wharf 3' and a digital overlay reading 'Mar 2026 | Northern Philippines'.
cross_modal_findings.explicit_caption_or_overlay: Subic Bay Wharf 3, Mar 2026 | Northern Philippines
cross_modal_findings.image_text_alignment_hint: The image contains explicit overlays ('Subic Bay', 'Mar 2026') that align with the post's claim of a specific time and location for the ship's arrival.

cross_modal_findings.post_claim_about_image: The post claims this is a banner for 'Japan-Philippines Joint Maritime Readiness 2026' in Palawan.
cross_modal_findings.what_image_shows: The image shows a large white banner on a building reading 'Japan-Philippines Joint Maritime Readiness 2026' with the text 'Palawan' in the top right corner.
cross_modal_findings.image_text_alignment_hint: The text on the banner explicitly confirms the event name and year cited in the post, and the location tag matches the post's claim.

cross_modal_findings.post_claim_about_image: The post uses this image to illustrate Japanese domestic political support or statements regarding the deployment.
cross_modal_findings.what_image_shows: The image shows a person speaking at a podium in a formal setting, likely a government or parliamentary session, with an audience seated behind.
cross_modal_findings.image_text_alignment_hint: The image provides generic visual context for 'political statements' but does not explicitly identify the speaker or the specific content of the speech mentioned in the text.

cross_modal_findings.post_claim_about_image: The post uses this image to illustrate the construction of new warships, noting an overlaid 'SSBC' crane.
cross_modal_findings.what_image_shows: The image shows a shipyard with a large red gantry crane bearing the text 'CSSC' (China State Shipbuilding Corporation) and Chinese characters, with a ship hull in the background.
cross_modal_findings.image_text_alignment_hint: The post claims this illustrates Japanese warship construction, but the image shows a CSSC (Chinese) shipyard crane, creating a potential discrepancy or implying a specific narrative about shipbuilding origins.

cross_modal_findings.post_claim_about_image: The post uses this image to show Japanese Self-Defense Forces soldiers conducting training near a helicopter.
cross_modal_findings.what_image_shows: The image shows soldiers in camouflage gear lying on the ground near a large military helicopter with a Japanese flag roundel on the side.
cross_modal_findings.image_text_alignment_hint: The image visually supports the claim of Japanese military training activities, showing equipment and personnel consistent with the Self-Defense Forces.

retrieved_evidence.text: 日本自卫队近年来多次参与与菲律宾武装部队在吕宋岛北部和巴拉望等沿岸地区的联合演训，活动多以两栖登陆、人道援助和灾害救援等名义展开。据相关人员介绍，此类演训通常在傍晚或清晨进行，日方两栖装甲车、登陆艇从近海向海滩推进，身着自卫队迷彩的人员与菲律宾士兵协同上岸，周边可见标注“Cagayan”或“Palawan”的道路标志以及悬挂菲律宾国旗的设施，以体现合作地点和主权归属。
日本与菲律宾安全合作的制度化进程近年明显加快。除传统的防务对话外，两国正推动签署“互惠准入协定”（RAA），以便简化日本自卫队人员和装备进入菲律宾境内进行训练和救灾的程序。根据外交与防务部门公开信息，双方还计划在情报共享、海上监视和人道援助演练方面深化合作，有研究者指出，这些安排未来可能演变为轮换部署或长期驻训的法律和程序基础，与自卫队在菲律宾设立常态化驻扎点的外界推测密切相关。
围绕日本自卫队可能在菲律宾建立常态化驻扎点的问题，外界关注焦点之一是日本海上自卫队舰艇频繁造访菲律宾港口。近年来有报道提及，日方舰艇靠泊苏比克湾、马尼拉等地港口开展访问和补给，岸边往往悬挂菲律宾国旗，并设有写明港口名称的标识牌。军方人士指出，此类靠泊过程中，甲板物资装卸、补给车队进出港区的场景，已成为日菲安全合作逐步常态化的重要象征，被视作未来基地轮驻安排的前奏。
日本国内船厂为海上自卫队建造新舰艇，下水和命名仪式一般在造船厂码头举行，舰身喷涂“JS”等字样。近年来，随着对菲律宾安全合作的重视，有分析报道称，日方在展示新型护卫舰、巡逻舰时，刻意强调其适用于南海和巴士海峡一线的远海部署任务。相关仪式中，舰艏挂彩旗、官员致辞与礼炮环节，常被解读为日本在推进“联合海上戒备演训”的物质基础，外界据此推测这些舰艇未来可能参与在菲律宾周边海域的联合行动或兵力轮换。

在日本国内安全政策转向的过程中，自卫队正尝试将“人道援助与灾害救援”（HA/DR）作为扩展海外行动空间的重要标签。东日本大地震及周边国家频发的台风、地震灾害，使得日本社会对自卫队海外救灾任务持较高接受度。近年来，日本在菲律宾参加多场救援演练，内容包括工程支援、医疗救护和物资空投。根据防务官员的说法，这类行动一方面展示“非战斗”形象，另一方面也为部队熟悉当地地形、港口设施和指挥体系提供机会，与未来可能的登陆部署和基地轮驻形成实践上的连续性。
据日本海上自卫队社交媒体上发布的最新消息称，在7月17日，日本海上自卫队最新一艘摩耶级导弹驱逐舰在横滨机子工厂中举行了下水仪式和命名仪式，这艘最新的摩耶级驱逐舰是同型号军舰的二号舰，舷号180，被命名为“羽黑（はぐろ）”号。日本自卫队宣称该舰将进一步提升日本的防空能力。而该舰的舰名也来 ​​​

美国在菲律宾的军事布局，为日本自卫队可能的轮驻和登陆演练提供了重要参照。苏比克湾、克拉克等地曾是美军基地，近年来又在EDCA框架下开放更多设施供美军使用。安全分析人士认为，日本如果在菲律宾采取类似“轮换进驻”“联合使用基地”的模式，往往会优先选择已有盟军基础设施的地点，以便利用现成港口、机场和后勤体系。日本与美国在情报、通信和指挥体系上的高度兼容，也为日菲美三边演训和潜在联合部署提供了操作层面的便利。

区域军备竞赛在讨论日本自卫队与菲律宾合作时常被一并提及。中国海军“福建舰”等大型航母和新型两栖攻击舰的建造与试验活动，引发邻近国家对西太平洋和南海力量平衡的关注。根据地区安全研究机构的分析，随着周边国家提升远海投送能力，日本也在强化自身防卫力量部署，并通过与菲律宾、美国等开展联合海上训练和港口访问来拓展战略纵深。这种多国海上力量的相互观望与扩充，被视为推动日本在菲律宾探索更稳定存在形式的重要背景因素之一。
本地区安全事务观察人士近日指出，近年来中菲、日菲以及美菲在海上安全、联合演训和舰艇互访等方面的合作活动呈现增加趋势。菲律宾国防部公开的简报和年度计划中，多次提及与多国海军开展海上联合演练、海上搜救及港口访问等安排，部分行动对媒体开放。据分析，这类互动被视为区域安全合作升温的一个侧面，具体行动规模与频率则依各方外交与防务协调情况有所变化。
菲律宾国家减灾机构有关负责人近日表示，由于群岛地理位置特殊，多地长期面临台风、地震和火山活动等自然灾害风险。该负责人回顾称，历年来在多次重大台风登陆和地震发生后，菲律宾曾在联合国与地区机制框架下接受多国军队或民事机构参与的人道主义援助和灾害救援协调行动。观察人士指出，这类跨国救援通常包括物资空运、工程抢险和医疗支援等内容，相关具体安排视灾情及外交协商确定。
据日本防卫政策研究人士介绍，自卫队海外派遣在国内政治与法律层面一直较为敏感，相关行动通常被严格限定在特定框架下进行。防卫省近年来在对外说明时，多以训练交流、舰艇访问、后勤协作及人道主义救援等名义介绍相关活动，并强调遵守现行法律和国会授权程序。消息人士指出，这种表述方式旨在在推进国际安全合作的同时，回应国内社会对于自卫队任务范围与角色扩展的持续关注。

在菲律宾国内，关于是否允许日本自卫队扩大存在度的讨论同样复杂。菲律宾宪法对外国军队驻扎持谨慎态度，但近年来在南海局势和灾害频发的背景下，政府相继与美国推进强化防务合作协定（EDCA），并考虑与日本建立类似框架。当地学者指出，政府在向公众解释日菲联合演训和人道援助时，往往强调救灾、海上巡逻和能力建设功能，同时试图淡化“驻军”色彩，以平衡主权敏感与安全需求之间的矛盾，这也影响着外界对日本在菲长期部署可能性的判断。

地缘战略层面，日本自卫队在菲律宾附近的存在与所谓“第一岛链”部署构想紧密相连。战略研究机构普遍认为，从琉球群岛、台湾到吕宋和巴拉望构成的弧线，是日本及其盟友关注的关键防卫线。通过在菲律宾北部和西部沿海开展联合演训和人道援助，日本可在和平时期提前布局后勤补给、海空监视及情报共享网络。关係专家指出，这种存在形式即使以训练和轮驻名义展开，也会在客观上形成一定的常态化驻扎效果，从而影响南海和台湾周边的整体力量对比。
日本政府与安全研究领域人士近年来在多种场合谈及台湾周边局势时，多次提到台湾海峡对本国能源与货物航运通道的重要性。据熟悉政策辩论的学者介绍，在国会质询、智库报告以及媒体访谈中，部分观点将这一海域形容为“日本的生命线”，相关表述随后在舆论场被广泛转引，引发对于海上交通安全与地区局势的争论。有评论认为，这类说法在国内安全政策讨论中已成为一个具有象征意味的背景议题。
据日本防卫省相关人士近日透露，日本海上自卫队正在按中长期舰队建设规划，推进包括摩耶级在内的多型新型宙斯盾驱逐舰后续舰建造计划。有关造船企业的公开信息显示，部分新舰艇已经完成船台建造并举行下水与命名仪式，过程向媒体开放。防卫省官员称，新舰艇将用于加强远海防空与情报监视能力，具体服役时间和部署细节将视测试进展及预算安排陆续公布。

福建舰提前一天返航，又一艘巨舰传出新消息，为改变战争规则而生

国产首艘电磁弹射航母福建舰的首次海试工作已经进入尾声，同时在建的076两栖攻击舰也在近期曝安装甲板外飘结构的照片。
这些信息的披露让外界普遍认为中国大陆有望在2027年前就服役装备福建舰与076首舰，而在台当局看来这显然不是什么 ​​​

关于“台湾海峡是否是日本的生命线”的争论，近年来在日本社交媒体和舆论场中持续升温。关係人士指出，随着日本政府在《国家安全保障战略》中频繁提及台湾海峡和平稳定的重要性，众多网民、评论者在平台上转发相关言论，讨论日本海上交通线、南西诸岛防卫以及与美国、菲律宾协作的问题。这类舆论材料往往将台湾海峡局势与日本能源进口和海上贸易直接挂钩，为自卫队加强在菲律宾一带联合演训、人道援助与基地轮驻预案提供了安全焦虑与政策转向的社会氛围。

台湾海峡真的是日本“生命线”吗？

日本官方和学者经常强调台湾海峡对于日本国际航运的重要性，好像只要台海有事日本就活不下去一样，事实真是如此吗？
中国台湾学者张竞近期撰写了一篇文章，指出这个很明显的谎言。
在介绍文章内容之前，我们不妨回顾一下日本议员在提问高市早苗时的“完整版对话”— ​​​

The post claims this is a 'cover photo' of a Japanese amphibious vehicle landing in Northern Philippines in late March 2026, with a 'CAGAYAN' sign in the background.
The image shows amphibious assault vehicles landing on a beach with soldiers disembarking; a Philippine flag is in the foreground and a sign reading 'CAGAYAN' is visible in the background.
The post claims this shows a Japanese warship arriving at a Philippine port in March 2026, with an overlaid sign reading 'Subic Bay Wharf 3'.
The image shows a grey warship in a harbor with a wooden sign in the foreground reading 'Subic Bay Wharf 3' and a digital overlay reading 'Mar 2026 | Northern Philippines'.
Subic Bay Wharf 3, Mar 2026 | Northern Philippines
The post claims this is a banner for 'Japan-Philippines Joint Maritime Readiness 2026' in Palawan.
The image shows a large white banner on a building reading 'Japan-Philippines Joint Maritime Readiness 2026' with the text 'Palawan' in the top right corner.
Japan-Philippines Joint Maritime Readiness 2026, Palawan
The post uses this image to illustrate Japanese domestic political support or statements regarding the deployment.
The image shows a person speaking at a podium in a formal setting, likely a government or parliamentary session, with an audience seated behind.
The post uses this image to illustrate the construction of new warships, noting an overlaid 'SSBC' crane.
The image shows a shipyard with a large red gantry crane bearing the text 'CSSC' (China State Shipbuilding Corporation) and Chinese characters, with a ship hull in the background.
The post uses this image to show Japanese Self-Defense Forces soldiers conducting training near a helicopter.
The image shows soldiers in camouflage gear lying on the ground near a large military helicopter with a Japanese flag roundel on the side.

text_analysis.alignment_reasoning: The post fabricates a specific timeline and deployment plan for Japanese Self-Defense Forces in the Philippines, and presents manipulated images as evidence of events that have not occurred.
text_analysis.t1_evidence: The post claims a specific 'supplementary arrangement' exists with a timeline of 'April late dispatch of 80-120 people' and 'June main force of 600-1000 people', but evidence [2] only mentions general trends of increased cooperation without any such specific agreement, numbers, or schedule.
t1_problematic_subclaims: 日菲双方围绕一份“补充安排”已完成关键条款磋商
首批先遣分队预计在4月下旬抵达，到了6月，会出现以“演训”为名的主力科目集中展开
text_analysis.t2_evidence: The post frames general diplomatic and military cooperation trends as a concrete, imminent 'countdown' to 'landing deployment' (登陆部署), whereas evidence [2] and [4] describe these activities as routine joint exercises, port visits, and disaster relief coordination within existing legal frameworks.
t2_problematic_subclaims: 自卫队“登陆菲律宾”进入倒计时
自卫队登陆菲律宾已经被写进日菲合作的实施进度表
text_analysis.t3_evidence: The post presents images with overlaid text claiming events in 'March 2026' in the Philippines (e.g., Image 1 'Cagayan', Image 2 'Subic Bay'), but evidence [1] confirms the 'Haguro' destroyer naming ceremony occurred on July 17 in Yokohama, Japan, indicating the images are either misdated, mislocated, or fabricated composites presented as current/recent reality.
t3_problematic_subclaims: 图0：2026年3月下旬，菲律宾北部沿岸...先遣分队抵达
图2：...叠加“SUBIC”字样港口牌并加上“2026年3月 菲律宾”水印
text_analysis.c1_evidence: Image 5 shows a shipyard with a crane labeled 'CSSC' (China State Shipbuilding Corporation) and '沪东中华' ( Hudong-Zhonghua), which is a Chinese shipbuilder, but the post text claims it illustrates 'Japanese Maritime Self-Defense Force new surface combatant construction' and overlays it with 'SSBC' (likely a typo for CSSC or misinterpretation) to support a narrative about Japanese military expansion.
c1_problematic_subclaims: 图5：一张明显拍摄于日本国内船厂的下水仪式照片...背景有龙门吊...叠加“SUBIC”字样
海自新型驱逐舰抵菲前沿点执行协同任务
text_analysis.global_assessment: The post is a highly misleading fabrication that invents a specific deployment timeline and uses manipulated images (including Chinese shipyard footage) to support a false narrative of imminent Japanese military landing in the Philippines.

images.scene_description: A high-fidelity, cinematic shot of amphibious vehicles and soldiers landing on a tropical beach with a Philippine flag and a 'CAGAYAN' sign. The lighting is perfectly uniform, the water physics are stylized, and the composition is too perfect for a candid news photo.
images.v1_evidence: The image exhibits the 'high-fidelity AI press-photo' signature: flawless lighting, plastic-like skin textures on soldiers, and a hyper-real, commercial render aesthetic that lacks the imperfections of a real camera capture.
images.explanation: Flagged as V1 due to the synthetic, AI-generated quality of the 'candid' military scene.

images.scene_description: A Japanese warship in a shipyard with a wooden sign reading 'Subic Bay Wharf 3' and a news-style banner 'Mar 2026 | Northern Philippines' overlaid at the bottom.
images.ocr_text: Subic Bay Wharf 3 | Mar 2026 | Northern Philippines | 自衛艦命名式
images.v2_evidence: The image contains a large graphical banner overlay at the bottom and a wooden sign that appears digitally inserted or composited to create a false location context.
images.explanation: Flagged as V2 and C2 because the image is a shipyard scene edited with overlays to falsely depict a port arrival in the Philippines.

images.scene_description: A large white banner on a building reading 'Japan-Philippines Joint Maritime Readiness 2026' with a small 'Palawan' text in the corner.
images.v1_evidence: The banner text is perfectly straight and crisp against a curved, textured wall, and the lighting on the banner does not match the ambient light of the building, suggesting a synthetic or heavily manipulated graphic.
images.v2_evidence: The banner text 'Japan-Philippines Joint Maritime Readiness 2026' appears to be digitally superimposed onto the building facade to create a specific event narrative.
images.explanation: Flagged as V1 and V2 due to the synthetic nature of the banner and its digital insertion into the scene.

images.scene_description: A Japanese government official speaking at a podium in a formal setting, likely a parliament or committee room.
A distant view of a shipyard with a large red crane. The text on the crane reads 'CSSC' (China State Shipbuilding Corporation) but is presented in the context of Japanese warship construction.
images.explanation: Flagged as C1 and C2 because the image depicts a Chinese shipyard while the text claims it illustrates Japanese warship construction.

images.scene_description: Japanese Self-Defense Forces soldiers lying on the ground near a large military helicopter.

image_analysis.global_notes: The post relies heavily on synthetic or heavily edited images (V1/V2) to fabricate a timeline of Japanese military deployment in the Philippines. Image 1 is a high-quality AI render, Image 2 and 3 have obvious digital overlays, and Image 5 uses a Chinese shipyard to misrepresent Japanese construction.

judge.level3_rationale: 该帖子捏造了日本自卫队向菲律宾派遣部队的具体时间表和“补充安排”，并大量使用AI生成图片、合成图片以及张冠李戴的中国船厂照片作为虚假证据，其核心主张完全缺乏事实依据，属于典型的虚假信息。
judge.reasoning_trace: The post contains fabricated specific claims (T1) about a deployment timeline. The images are either AI-generated (V1), edited composites (V2), or semantically inconsistent (C1 - Chinese shipyard for Japanese ships). With T1, V1, V2, and 4+ companion labels, the verdict is False.
judge.core_claim: 日本自卫队已制定具体时间表，将于2026年4月至6月分阶段向菲律宾吕宋北部和巴拉望派遣先遣队及主力部队进行登陆部署。
key_subclaims: 日菲双方已完成关于自卫队登陆部署的“补充安排”关键条款磋商。
首批先遣分队（80-120人）预计于2026年4月下旬抵达菲律宾。
主力演训部队（600-1000人）将于2026年6月进入菲律宾。
图0展示了2026年3月自卫队在菲律宾卡加延登陆的现场画面。
图2展示了日本海自新型驱逐舰在菲律宾苏比克湾的部署情况。
图5展示了日本国内船厂为海自建造新舰的场景。

subclaim_findings.subclaim: 日菲双方已完成关于自卫队登陆部署的“补充安排”关键条款磋商。
subclaim_findings.note: 证据仅提及RAA（互惠准入协定）的推进和一般性合作，未证实存在具体的“登陆部署补充安排”或已完成的秘密条款。

subclaim_findings.subclaim: 首批先遣分队（80-120人）预计于2026年4月下旬抵达菲律宾。
subclaim_findings.note: 证据未提及任何具体的派遣时间表或人数，该细节属于凭空捏造。

subclaim_findings.subclaim: 主力演训部队（600-1000人）将于2026年6月进入菲律宾。
subclaim_findings.note: 同上，无证据支持这一具体的未来部署计划。

subclaim_findings.subclaim: 图0展示了2026年3月自卫队在菲律宾卡加延登陆的现场画面。
subclaim_findings.note: 图片具有AI生成特征（光影完美、细节模糊），且证据指出此类演训通常发生在傍晚或清晨，与图片中的正午光照不符，且无官方记录证实该特定事件。

subclaim_findings.subclaim: 图2展示了日本海自新型驱逐舰在菲律宾苏比克湾的部署情况。
subclaim_findings.note: 图片明显经过编辑，将日本国内船厂的命名仪式照片与“Subic Bay”路牌及“Mar 2026”字幕合成，属于视觉造假。

subclaim_findings.subclaim: 图5展示了日本国内船厂为海自建造新舰的场景。
subclaim_findings.note: 图片中的龙门吊清晰显示“CSSC”（中国船舶集团）和“沪东中华”字样，这是中国船厂，与文本声称的日本船厂完全矛盾。

judge.verdict_reasoning: The post fabricates a specific timeline and deployment plan (T1) for Japanese forces in the Philippines, which is not supported by evidence. It uses AI-generated images (V1) and heavily edited composites (V2) to create false visual evidence of these events. Furthermore, it misuses a photo of a Chinese shipyard to represent Japanese shipbuilding (C1/C2). The combination of T1, V1, V2, and multiple cross-modal inconsistencies (7 labels total) clearly meets the criteria for 'False'.

image_findings.explanation: Image exhibits high-fidelity AI generation characteristics (uncanny lighting, generic details) and is presented as a real event photo from March 2026, which is fabricated.
Image is a composite with overlaid text ('Subic Bay', 'Mar 2026') and a tablet, misrepresenting a Japanese ship naming ceremony as a deployment in the Philippines.
The banner text 'Japan-Philippines Joint Maritime Readiness 2026' appears artificially inserted or generated, as no such specific event banner is corroborated by evidence.
Image shows a Japanese official, consistent with the general topic of political discussion, though the specific context is vague.
Image shows a Chinese shipyard (CSSC/Hudong-Zhonghua) but is used to illustrate Japanese shipbuilding, creating a direct semantic and contextual contradiction.
Image shows Japanese military equipment, which is topically relevant but does not support the specific fabricated deployment claims.
